note.summary: With 53 settled trades, DOWN direction, trending/hurst, zscore -2..1, ttc 120-240s, bb_normal, adx_strong, mtf_bearish_aligned, cvd_sell_pressure, and range_middle show confirmed positive EV and win-rates above breakeven with n>=8; overall pnl modestly positive but many regimes remain mixed or negative. Patterns in bb_normal (wr 0.875) and mtf_bearish_aligned (wr 0.833) stand out as robust while active volume, htf_bullish, and bb_squeeze/expansion are failing. No prior analysis available so all observations are new.
warnings: Total n=53 still small; many buckets near n=8-20 threshold
pnl and ev signs conflict in several regimes indicating cost or variance effects
observe_only mode means no live validation yet
changes_since_last: Initial analysis; no prior results to compare
focus_next: Accumulate samples specifically in bb_normal, mtf_bearish_aligned, cvd_sell_pressure and zscore -2..1
Monitor whether normal bb_state and strong adx continue to separate from expansion/squeeze

history.summary: With 53 settled trades the bot shows modest positive EV (overall wr 0.604, avg_ev 0.116) driven by a handful of buckets that meet n>=8, wr comfortably above breakeven and positive realized pnl; DOWN_STRONG, trending/Hurst, BB-normal, bearish_mtf_aligned, sell_pressure/CVD and 120-240s TTC stand out as confirmed. Most other slices remain too small or show wr <=0.55 with negative pnl and are still noise. No prior analysis exists so all observations are new.

With only 53 settled trades, DOWN_STRONG signals in trending regimes (esp. 120-240s TTC, normal BB state, bearish-aligned MTF, sell-pressure CVD, strong ADX) show confirmed positive EV and win-rates well above breakeven after costs; UP signals and most expansions/squeezes remain marginal or negative. Many buckets still fall below the n>=8 threshold and are ignored as noise. Sample remains too small for robust conclusions.
changes_since_last: No prior_analysis supplied; baseline established from first 53 trades

history.summary: With 53 settled trades, DOWN_STRONG signals in trending regimes, 120-240s TTC, normal BB state, sell_pressure CVD, and bearish_aligned MTF show confirmed positive EV (0.11-0.14) and win-rates 0.65-0.83 above breakeven with n>=12; overall sample remains small and selective (only 53/39189 accepted). UP_STRONG and long-TTC (>=240s) buckets are failing with negative realized PnL despite marginal EV. No material changes possible on first analysis; focus must stay strictly observe-only.

With 53 settled trades (n>=8 filter applied), DOWN_STRONG signals in trending regimes, normal BB state, 120-240s TTC, z-score -2..1, sell_pressure CVD, bearish MTF alignment, and strong ADX show confirmed positive EV (wr 0.64-0.83, avg_ev 0.11-0.14 after costs). UP signals, >=240s TTC, expansion/squeeze BB, and active volume buckets are weaker or breakeven-negative. Sample remains small and observe-only; no material change possible without prior_analysis.
changes_since_last: first analysis; no prior baseline available

history.summary: With n=53 total settled trades, overall win-rate 60.4% and positive avg EV after cost (0.116) confirm mild edge, driven almost entirely by DOWN_STRONG signals in trending regimes. Buckets with n>=8 show confirmed profitability in DOWN direction, bearish-aligned MTF, normal BB state, 120-240s TTC, strong ADX, sell-pressure CVD and range-middle; UP signals and >=240s TTC remain negative or marginal. Most other slices have n<8 and are ignored as unreliable.

With n=53 settled trades, overall edge is modestly positive (wr 0.604, ev_after_cost 0.116) but driven by a handful of regimes; DOWN_STRONG, trending/Hurst, normal BB, strong ADX, sell-pressure CVD, bearish MTF alignment, and 120-240s TTC show confirmed positive EV with n>=8 and wr well above breakeven. Most other buckets are either n<8 noise or show flat/negative realized pnl despite positive ev. Sample remains too small for high-confidence live deployment.

With n=53 total settled signals, overall win rate 60.4% and small positive EV after costs, but only a handful of buckets meet n>=8 with win rates confidently above breakeven and positive realized PnL. DOWN_STRONG, trending/Hurst, z-score -2..1, TTC 120-240s, BB normal, volume dead, bearish HTF/aligned, strong ADX, sell-pressure CVD, and range-middle/bottom show confirmed edge; most other slices are noise or under-sampled. No material changes possible as this is the first analysis.

With n=53 total settled trades, DOWN_STRONG signals in trending regimes show confirmed positive EV (wr 0.69, +15.5 pnl) while UP signals remain marginal/negative; strong patterns include ttc 120-240s, bb normal, bearish_aligned mtf, sell_pressure cvd, and zscore -2..1 with n>=13 and positive pnl/ev after costs. Many buckets remain too small or mixed to confirm, with overall edge still modest and sample-limited.

With only 53 settled trades, overall win rate 60.4% and positive avg EV after cost (0.116) but results remain fragile; confirmed edges appear in DOWN_STRONG (n=26, wr=0.692), trending regime (n=50), normal BB state (n=16, wr=0.875), 120-240s TTC (n=26), bearish-aligned MTF (n=12), sell-pressure CVD (n=19) and strong-trend ADX (n=31) where wr comfortably exceeds breakeven and EV>0. Most other buckets are either n<8, show negative realized pnl despite decent wr, or are too close to breakeven to confirm.

With n=53 total settled trades, DOWN_STRONG (n=26, WR=0.692, EV=0.136) and bearish-aligned MTF (n=12, WR=0.833) plus sell-pressure CVD (n=19, WR=0.737) and normal BB state (n=16, WR=0.875) buckets show confirmed positive EV after costs and WR above plausible breakeven; trending/Hurst and 120-240s TTC also hold with n>=20. UP direction and >=240s TTC remain negative or marginal. All other buckets ignored due to n<8.
changes_since_last: first analysis; sample grew to 53 settled trades with DOWN_STRONG and bearish MTF emerging as clearest edges

history.summary: With 53 settled trades overall WR 60.4% and +1.35 USD pnl (+0.116 EV after cost), confirmed profitable patterns (n>=8) center on trending/Hurst, normal BB state, 120-240s TTC, strong ADX, sell-pressure CVD, bearish-aligned MTF, and DOWN_STRONG signals; these show WR confidently above breakeven with positive EV. Many other buckets remain below n=8 and are ignored as noise. No prior analysis available so no changes tracked yet.

With n=53 settled trades, DOWN_STRONG (n=26, WR 0.692), trending regime (n=50, WR 0.64), 120-240s TTC (n=26, WR 0.731), normal BB state (n=16, WR 0.875), bearish_aligned MTF (n=12, WR 0.833), and sell_pressure CVD (n=19, WR 0.737) show confirmed positive EV after costs with n>=8 and WR comfortably above breakeven. UP signals, >=240s holds, active volume, and expansion/squeeze BB states remain negative or marginal. Sample still small overall; most other buckets ignored due to n<8.

With n=53 total settled trades, the bot shows modest positive EV (overall wr 0.60, avg_ev 0.116) concentrated in a few regimes with n>=8; DOWN_STRONG, trending/Hurst, normal BB state, bearish MTF alignment, sell-pressure CVD, 120-240s TTC, and dead-volume buckets are the clearest positive-EV patterns while UP and most expansion/squeeze states are flat-to-negative.

With n=53 settled trades, DOWN_STRONG signals in trending regimes (esp. bearish-aligned MTF, sell-pressure CVD, normal BB state, 120-240s TTC, strong ADX) show confirmed positive EV (wr 0.65-0.83, aggregate pnl +15 to +30 USD) while UP signals and long holds remain marginal or negative. Sample still small; only buckets n>=8 considered, with DOWN_STRONG, bearish supertrend, and range-middle emerging as clearest edges above breakeven after costs.

With 53 settled trades, confirmed profitable patterns center on DOWN_STRONG signals in trending regimes (n=50, wr=0.64), normal BB state (n=16, wr=0.875), 120-240s TTC (n=26, wr=0.731), bearish MTF alignment (n=12, wr=0.833), and sell-pressure CVD (n=19, wr=0.737), all showing positive EV after costs and n>=8. Many other buckets remain below reliable thresholds or show mixed/negative results despite adequate samples. No prior analysis provided so changes are not assessable.
changes_since_last: First analysis; sample of 53 now available for initial pattern detection

history.summary: With 53 settled trades, confirmed positive EV edges exist in DOWN_STRONG signals (n=26, wr=0.692), trending regimes (n=50), z-score -2..1, 120-240s TTC (n=26, wr=0.731), BB normal state (n=16, wr=0.875), dead volume, strong ADX, and bearish-aligned MTF, all with wr confidently above breakeven and positive avg_ev_after_cost. UP signals, >=240s holds, active volume, and expansion/squeeze BB states show negative or marginal realized pnl despite some ev. Sample remains small overall; many buckets ignored due to n<8.

With 53 settled trades, DOWN_STRONG signals in trending regimes show confirmed edge (0.69 WR, +15.5 pnl, EV 0.136) while UP_STRONG remains marginal; normal BB state, 120-240s TTC, bearish-aligned MTF, and sell-pressure CVD also clear n>=8 thresholds with positive EV. Many other buckets (zscore extremes, range states, volume active) are still noisy or negative despite larger n. Sample remains too small for live deployment.
changes_since_last: first analysis, no prior; sample grew from 0 to 53 settled trades

history.summary: With n=53 total settled trades, confirmed positive EV patterns (n>=8, wr>breakeven, positive pnl) center on DOWN_STRONG signals in trending/Hurst regimes, normal BB state, dead volume, strong ADX trend, bearish MTF alignment, CVD sell pressure, zscore -2..1, and 120-240s TTC. UP signals and >=240s holds show negative realized pnl despite positive avg EV. Sample remains too small for most sub-buckets.

With only 53 settled trades, overall win rate 0.604 and tiny positive EV (0.116) after costs, a handful of buckets with n>=8 now show confirmed edge: DOWN direction/strong signals, trending/Hurst, 120-240s TTC, normal BB state, dead volume, strong ADX trend, bearish-aligned MTF, sell-pressure CVD, and range-middle/bottom all deliver win rates 0.65-0.83 with positive realized PnL. Most other slices remain below reliable thresholds or show negative PnL despite decent EV. Sample is still too thin for live deployment.

With 53 settled trades, DOWN direction, trending/hurst, zscore -2..1, ttc 120-240s, bb_normal, adx_strong, mtf_bearish_aligned, cvd_sell_pressure, and range_middle show confirmed positive EV and win-rates above breakeven with n>=8; overall pnl modestly positive but many regimes remain mixed or negative. Patterns in bb_normal (wr 0.875) and mtf_bearish_aligned (wr 0.833) stand out as robust while active volume, htf_bullish, and bb_squeeze/expansion are failing. No prior analysis available so all observations are new.
changes_since_last: Initial analysis; no prior results to compare

headlines: BTC at ~$59.6K with 24h drop ~2%, 7d ~7%; testing sub-$60K levels
Spot BTC ETFs saw major outflows incl. IBIT -$528M single day (part of ~$4.4B June bleed); ETH also outflowing
XRP ETF +$15.6M inflow June 26 contrasts BTC/ETH outflows of hundreds of millions

note.summary: Paper bot shows 62.9% win rate across 124 settled trades with +$39.74 PnL but 1.18 profit factor and asymmetric losses (avg loss $4.67 vs avg win $3.27). Only 7 trades in confidence-tier system (5 tier-B, 2 tier-C) with tiny n's: trending regime 4/4 wins but insufficient sample. Most edge comes from 'underdog_price_below_floor' rejections (213/220), suggesting mispricing detectorânot directional skillâdrives returns. No robust 5-min directional edge demonstrated.

knob_recommendations.reason: Current tier-B buckets have n=1-5; require â¥30 to claim any real edge
Avg loss 1.43Ã avg win; cap max loss at 0.7Ã avg win to fix asymmetry
213/220 rejects are underdog floor hits; tighten -2bp to test if mispricing edge persists

new_lessons.rule: Require profit factor â¥1.5 before any knob expansion; 1.18 is break-even after real costs
Confidence tier buckets need nâ¥30 to be actionable; current n=1-5 are noise
Edge appears to be underdog mispricing detection (213 floor rejects), not 5-min directional prediction
Avg loss 43% larger than avg win destroys compounding; must cap or abandon strategy

lessons.rule: TradingView 2-4min timeframe signals have no predictive edge on 5min BTC outcomes (45% vs 39% base); treat as noise, not signal
DOWN win rate (63.4%) is higher than UP (50%), but DOWN trades were 4x more frequentâthis is filter selection bias, not a tradable DOWN edge
168/277 execution gate rejections for underdog_price_below_floor indicate edge model outputs sub-viable edges; tighten model threshold or raise gate floor
Profit factor 1.0007 with avg_loss > avg_win means any transaction cost increase or slippage wipes edge; do not trade until PF > 1.05 on 500+ samples
TradingView MTF signal hit rate 45.3% (vs 38.8% base) is statistically zero; do NOT trade on directional convictionâedge is mispricing arbitrage only
DOWN: 63.9% win, +$7.71 PnL (n=83); UP: 50% win, -$6.35 PnL (n=20). Asymmetry is not alphaâit's market structure (underdog floor rejects 168 UP candidates). Exploit DOWN underdog tail only.
Brier 0.243 < baseline 0.25 but log-loss 0.683 is poor; edge_model calibration bins show no monotonic trend (0.3-0.4 bin: 31.4% empirical_up). Model is curve-fit noiseâdo NOT size by confidence.

168/175 rejects are underdog_price_below_floor; the gate is correctly filtering non-mispriced bets. The 103 accepted trades are *selection artifacts*âloosen floor to 0.25 cautiously and measure if win rate holds.
PF 1.01 on 103 trades is <1 std dev from breakeven (avg_loss $4.63 vs avg_win $2.98 suggests tail risk). Do NOT scale until PF â¥1.15 on â¥200 settled trades.
DOWN bets on 5-min BTC show 63.9% win vs 50% UP; exploit DOWN-only until UP win rate exceeds 58% over 50+ samples
TradingView UP signals hit 29%, DOWN hit 59%; treat UP signals as contrary indicator (fade or avoid) and DOWN as weak confirmation
168 UP rejections for underdog_price_below_floor vs 0 DOWN; underdog UP prices suffer adverse selection, tighten floor to 0.45+ for UP only
avg loss 1.55x avg win destroys compounding; reject any trade where model edge / (1 - model_prob) > 0.65 * (model_prob / model_edge)
profit factor 1.01 is coin-flip territory; require PF >= 1.15 sustained over 200+ trades before considering live capital allocation
DOWN signals (58.6% hit, +$7.71) have real edge; UP signals (29.2% hit, -$6.35) are anti-predictiveâtrade DOWN only
168/175 rejects for underdog_price_below_floor indicate systematic mispricing or overly conservative floor; investigate pricing vs realized outcomes
No-signal cohort (62% win, +$1.36) beats UP-signal cohort (54.2% win, -$6.35)âUP signals may be reverse indicators
5min timeframe DOWN signals: 5/7 hit (71.4% estimated), +$8.52âbest sub-context for exploitation
TradingView UP signals show 29% hit rate and -$6.35 PnL over 24 trades; disable UP signal consumption until nâ¥100 profitable sample
DOWN trades: 95 count, 63.2% WR vs UP: 20 count, 50% WRâpossible structural DOWN edge but sample imbalance (5:1) limits confidence
168/175 exec rejects = underdog_price_below_floor; edge model assigns >70% confidence to sub-0.20 underdog prices that fail realism
0.2-0.3 bucket: 54.5% empirical vs ~25% predicted; 0.3-0.4: 31.4% vs ~35%; recalibrate or reject edge_prob < 0.4
avg_loss $4.67 > avg_win $3.27. Asymmetric risk-reward warns of picking up pennies; tighten stops or raise conviction bar.
Down-side 64% WR (n=84) vs up 50% (n=20); investigate if DOWN setups have tighter spreads or better liquidity, or if gate logic systematically rejects weaker UP candidates
170 of 177 gate rejections are underdog_price_below_floor; if this kills profitable setups, consider dynamic floor tied to edge_quality or relax by 2 ticks
Avg loss $4.63 vs avg win $3.01 (1.54x); one bad streak erases multiple wins; add max_loss_per_trade knob or tighter stop-loss logic
All hurst/zscore/ttc buckets show n=1 or 'na'; either feature pipeline is broken or sample is too sparse to stratify; defer dimension-based rules until n>=15 per bucket
underdog_price_below_floor dominates rejects (170/177=96%); current floor too conservative, starving execution of mispriced opportunities
avg_loss ($4.63) > avg_win ($3.06); profit_factor 1.07 is fragileâstrategy needs 62%+ WR to break even, no margin for variance
Brier 0.242 â baseline 0.25, log-loss 0.68 high; model outputs probabilities but no exploitable mispricing vs marketârecalibration won't create edge
All dimension buckets have nâ¤2; 100% WR in these cells is noise, not signalârequire nâ¥30 per bucket before claiming contextual edge
85/105 DOWN trades, 64.7% WR DOWN vs 50% UP; asymmetric volume may exploit short-term mean reversion, but small win size suggests compression, not edge
underdog_price_below_floor killed 60% of gate flow; either floor is heroically saving you or choking alphaâneed A/B test
hurst=insufficient_data: 2/2 wins, +$11.11, but n=2 is noise; do NOT size up until n>=20
hurst_regime=trending: 0/1 (0% WR, -$5); avoid until 10+ samples prove otherwise
86 down vs 20 up fills; down_bias_gate passed 538 but imbalance suggests model or market skewâmonitor for drift
Brier=0.242 vs baseline=0.25 is marginal; log_loss=0.68 suggests overconfidenceâprobabilities need shrinkage
profit_factor=1.046 with avg_loss=$4.63 > avg_win=$3.06; one bad streak flips you negativeâno live until PF>1.2
max_drawdown=$43.62 vs net_pnl=$8.82 (5:1 risk/reward realized); unacceptable for liveâneed 3:1 or better
n=106 settled, but buckets have n=1-3; you need 500+ trades and >=30 per slice before claiming bucket-level edge
if execution_gate rejects >50% for underdog_price_below_floor, market data is stale or floor miscalibratedâinvestigate data feed before any floor adjustment
do not trade contexts with n<30; current max n=4 means all slice-level statistics are pure noise
avg_loss > avg_win (4.67 vs 3.21) with profit_factor 1.10 means win rate must stay >62% to break even; any drop in WR causes steep drawdown
profit_factor 1.02 is breakeven after real costs; need >=1.5 sustained over >=200 trades to consider live readiness
zscore=-1..1: 0% WR, -$10; mean-reversion absent in noise â block trades lacking divergence signal
hurst_regime=noise: 1 trade, 0% WR, -$5; randomness dominates â require trending or insufficient_data (2/2 wins)
ttc>=240s: 66.7% WR vs <240s poor; longer resolution windows allow signal to express vs noise decay
170/177 exec rejections = underdog_price_below_floor; tight floor protects but may hide mispriced tailsâmonitor
PF 1.06, avg_loss > avg_win; edge exists but RR poor â prioritize high-conviction over volume until PF>1.3
TradingView MTF(2m/3m/4m) signals are anti-predictive for 5min BTC: 45% hit rate vs 38% base; disable in edge model
DOWN directional edge confirmed at n=90: 64% win rate, +0.27 avg PnL vs UP n=20 50% win, -0.26 avg PnL
underdog_price_below_floor rejects 96% of execution candidates (176/183); re-calibrate floor or risk zero live flow
When external signal aligned WR < opposed WR by >5pp over nâ¥50, zero signal weight and audit feature polarity
When one direction loses money (PF<1.0) over nâ¥20 while other profits, disable losing side until 30-trade revalidation
When single reject reason >60% of gate rejects, relax that constraint by 10-15% if remaining EV>0 after costs
BTC 5min DOWN bets outperform UP by 11pp WR (+$14 delta over 110 trades); bias sizing/thresholds accordingly until n=500 retest
Do not slice performance by 9+ dimensions when total n=111; each bucket has nâ¤1, making all stats noise
DOWN side shows 64.8% WR vs UP 50%, but loss size 1.47x win size will erode edge under compounding
176/183 rejects are underdog_price_below_floor (96% of exec rejects); floor is primary bottleneck, not edge filter
Brier 0.239 vs baseline 0.25 is marginal; 0.2-0.3 bucket predicts 58% up actual, 0.3-0.4 predicts 29%, model miscalibrated
TradingView 2/3/4min BTC signals show 45% hit rate (worse than coin flip); bot wins more without them (64% vs 57%); ignore for trade direction
avg_loss $4.67 > avg_win $3.22 despite 61.5% win rate = fat left tail or adverse selection on losses
176/183 execution rejects were underdog_price_below_floor; this gate is preventing bad trades and must stay strict
91 down vs 20 up trades (4.6:1 ratio), 64.8% down win rate vs 50% up; DOWN side has sample-backed edge, UP does not
97% exec rejects = underdog floor; recalibrate or abandon underdog strategy
Single-trade context buckets (all n=1 here) provide zero statistical power; ignore exploit/avoid recommendations until nâ¥20 per bucket
Brier 0.240 vs baseline 0.250 shows model barely outperforms coin-flip; edge must emerge in subsets or model needs structural revision
93 down vs 20 up trades; investigate directional model asymmetry or market microstructure favoring short 5min BTC; rebalance training or add up-side feature calibration

underdog_price_below_floor rejected 97% of gate candidates (202/209); either model is miscalibrated or gate is overfitted to paper backtest; log rejected candidates' outcomes to measure opportunity cost
avg_loss $4.66 > avg_win $3.15 despite 61% win rate; risk/reward profile unsustainable; require min 1.2x win/loss ratio or tighten stop logic before scaling

all evaluated dimension buckets show 0% win rate or losses; edge model lacks predictive power in tested feature space; explore alternative features (funding, liquidations, CVD) or abandon 5min horizon
Brier 0.2399 vs baseline 0.25; model has minimal calibration advantage; directional forecasts are coin-flip quality; do not increase bet sizing until Brier <0.20 sustained over 500+ samples
20 UP vs 98 DOWN (1:5 ratio) signals directional filter or model bias; verify not discarding valid UP signals or systematically mispricing one side
All context buckets nâ¤2 with 0% WR are pure noiseânever trade contexts with <20 settled trades regardless of apparent WR
Profit factor 1.06 with avg_loss > avg_win is statistically indistinguishable from breakeven after any cost driftârequire avg_win â¥ 1.5Ã avg_loss
202/209 exec rejects at underdog floor suggests floor is primary filter, not feature modelâif >90% rejects at one gate, the gate IS the strategy
62% of execution candidates rejected for price_below_floor suggests floor is miscalibrated or true edge requires lower-priced bets
Every measured context slice (6/6) lost money; no context-conditional edge detectedâmay indicate model has no predictive power
Avg loss 47% larger than avg win while WR=61% yields PF=1.06; need WR>68% or cut losses 30% to sustain edge
Headline 61.2% WR driven by 30 legacy trades; 86 new fills contribute ~$0/trade avg PnL. Report WR separately for legacy vs. new accounting to avoid false confidence.
202/209 execution rejects = 96.7% due to underdog_price_below_floor. Either floor is miscalibrated or true edge lives above it; current gate prevents learning which.
All contexts with nâ¥4: WRâ¤50%, net negative PnL. No regime (Hurst/zscore/TTC/edge_quality) shows exploitable directional edge at 5-min horizon in current feature set.
Brier ~0.24, calibration_error 0.076 look OK, but derived from 5-trade buckets. Calibration metrics unreliable until nâ¥30 per bucket; do not trust current edge_model tuning.
PF=1.08 with avg_loss ($4.67) > avg_win ($3.19) and n=116 (30 legacy + 86 new) indicates survival bias or legacy cohort luck, not repeatable alpha. Require PFâ¥1.5 + loss<win to claim edge.
TradingView MTF signals show NO edge (45% hit) and OPPOSED trades win more (63% vs 57%); treat as noise or contrarian filter only
96/116 (82.8%) DOWN trades indicate severe imbalance; enforce UP/DOWN allocation caps to hedge regime shift
0.2-0.3 bucket empirical_up=58% (expect ~25%), 0.5-0.6 bucket empirical_up=50% (expect ~55%); model miscalibrated in tails
markov_state=stale_polymarket_down shows 100% win rate (n=1 tiny) + 202 underdog rejections = likely stale quote arb, not direction skill
up trades 50% win rate (n=20) vs down 63.9% (n=97); up side has no demonstrated edge
all context buckets n=1 except overall; cannot promote/demote contexts without >=30 samples per bucket
64% of execution candidates rejected (209/326), 97% due to underdog floor = gate is primary trade filter, not alpha model
Every dimension (regime, zscore, ttc, edge_quality, markov, spread, depth) shows n=1; no sample density to validate edgeâtreat all buckets as untested
DOWN side 97 trades, 63.9% WR, but avg_loss > avg_win and profit_factor=1.10; bias exists but execution quality poorâavoid conflating volume with edge
202/209 execution rejects for underdog_price_below_floor; gate is blocking 96.7% of flow post-feature-scoringâlikely miscalibrated or market structure mismatch
$43.62 max drawdown vs $21.70 net PnL; position sizing or stop discipline brokenâcurrent risk model unsustainable even in paper
If >60% of execution rejects share one reason (e.g., underdog_price_below_floor), investigate model bug or floor miscalibration before claiming selective filtering works
Profit factor <1.2 with avg_loss > avg_win means edge collapses under small cost/slippage changes; require PF >= 1.5 and avg_win >= avg_loss for robustness claim
Do not promote/demote contexts or claim subsegment edge with n<30; random walk can produce 100% WR on n=2 (2Ï ~ 35% probability)
UP trades (n=20, 50% WR) show no edge; DOWN trades (n=99, 64.6% WR) carry signal. Require DOWN confirmation or skip UP until n_UP>100.
207/214 execution rejects = underdog_price_below_floor. Market is efficient; bot must raise edge threshold or accept <5% fill rate.
PF=1.13, avg_loss=$4.67 > avg_win=$3.21. Risk/reward unfavorable; need WR>68% or better entry prices to sustain compounding.
Brier=0.238 vs baseline 0.25 (tiny improvement). Edge model buckets show scatter (0.3-0.4 predicts 30.6% UP, 0.5-0.6 predicts 50%). Needs 500+ samples for stability.
62% gate rejections for low price = bot chasing anti-edge; raise floor or rework signal
avg loss $4.67 vs win $3.21 â 62% WR yields only 1.10 PF; cap loss at 1.0x win or abandon
n=1 per bucket = zero statistical power; need 20+ per context to trust edge claims
bucket predicts 25% up, observes 61.5% (n=13) â model anticorrelated in low-prob tail
62% execution gate rejects for underdog_price_below_floor implies pricing model overestimates edge; require empirical win rate >65% at given price before lowering floor
all dimensional slices show n=1; require n>=20 per bucket before claiming context-specific edge
edge_model 0.2-0.3 bucket shows 61.5% empirical_up (inverted from model); investigate model feature weights or labeling errors
When 97% of execution rejects share one reason (underdog_price_below_floor=209/216), the gate is too strict to test the hypothesis; lower floor temporarily to gather evidence
Model's 0.2-0.3 predicted-up bin resolved 61.5% up (n=13); investigate feature polarity bugs or label errors before trusting low-confidence predictions
Profit factor 1.10 with avg_loss=$4.67 > avg_win=$3.21 implies win rate must stay >65% to remain profitable; any decay toward 50% will cause losses
hurst_regime=insufficient_data (n=1, 0% WR) and zscore_bucket=na (n=1, 0% WR) indicate missing feature computation; avoid trading when regime detection fails
edge_quality=high: n=2, 50% WR, -$0.57; label unreliable at small n
avg_loss > avg_win despite 62% WR = fragile; one bad streak kills edge
Brier 0.24 vs baseline 0.25, cal_error 0.089 OK, but sample inadequate
All context buckets n<=2; need 10x sample before context-specific rules
underdog_price_floor=0.20 rejects 97% of execution candidates; lower incrementally and monitor win_rate/PnL degradation to find optimal trade-off
hurst_regime=trending: 2/2 wins, +$8.21, but n=2 is anecdotal; require n>=20 in this regime before exploitation
markov_state=stale_polymarket_down: 0/1, -$5.00; avoid until n>=10 and win_rate>0.55
n=122 insufficient to claim edge in 5-min BTC; require n>=500 with profit_factor>1.5 and avg_loss<avg_win before live trading
If >95% of execution rejects share one reason, the gate preceding it is miscalibratedâfix upstream candidate generation or the threshold is a de-facto kill switch
When one direction represents >80% of volume and shows materially better win rate, assume temporary regime fit, not structural edgeârequire balanced sampling
3 settled trades in current accounting window vs 122 total indicates reporting stitched legacy dataâdo not infer parameter efficacy from pre-accounting trades; restart sample clock
Edge model calibration buckets with n<30 (e.g., 0.5-0.6 bucket n=14, 0.6-0.7 n=1) produce noise not signalâsuppress reporting or trading on buckets below statistical significance
hurst_regime=trending: 4/4 wins, +$17.24; insufficient_data: 0/1 wins, -$5. Require hurst confidence before trade.
markov_state=stale_polymarket_down: 0/1 wins, -$5. Block this state; stale + down = informed fast money.
zscore_bucket=na (1 trade, 0% WR, -$5) = missing divergence data. Gate on valid z-score.
0.3-0.4 bucket: n=50, empirical_up=0.30 (model predicts ~0.35 implicit). Model slightly overconfident in low-prob scenarios.
Only 5 'high edge_quality' trades. Need >=100 in exploit contexts before claiming statistical edge.
Require profit factor â¥1.5 before any knob expansion; 1.18 is break-even after real costs
Confidence tier buckets need nâ¥30 to be actionable; current n=1-5 are noise
Edge appears to be underdog mispricing detection (213 floor rejects), not 5-min directional prediction
